what_it_does: Defines a user systemd service that launches the IBM Granite Speech 4.0 1b transformer-based speech-to-text ear on GPU, exposing it on port 2033, with an optional environment file and restart-on-failure behavior.
observations: Service name identifies it as the 'Company STT ear' using IBM Granite Speech 4.0 1b
Runs via a Python virtual environment at ~/.voice-venvs/granite/bin/python
Comment notes ~4.66 GB resident VRAM and warns not to enable all three STT ears together due to a 16 GB ceiling
Uses %h (home directory) expansion and also hardcodes /home/tim in the script path
standouts: Explicit VRAM budget and arbitration note instructing selective enablement of STT ears
Mentions both a transformers path and a GGUF build as a lean alternative
600-second startup timeout for a model-loading service
conventions: Uses Type=simple and logs to journal
Uses %h for user-home paths in WorkingDirectory and EnvironmentFile
Optional env-file prefix '-' allows the service to start even if the file is missing
concerns: Mixed path conventions: %h expansion alongside hardcoded /home/tim in ExecStart
No explicit GPU device binding or memory limit in the unit
GGUF alternative is mentioned only in a comment, not reflected in the service command
Long TimeoutStartSec could mask startup hangs
notes: This is the user-level systemd unit for the Granite transformer STT ear, intended to be enabled only when VRAM arbitration allows it.
questions: How is the VRAM-arbitration decision made and by which component?
What are the other two STT ears and their resource requirements?
What variables are expected in voice.env?
Is there a separate service file for the GGUF build?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file content: it configures a systemd service to run the Granite STT ear on port 2033.
apparent_themes: VRAM budgeting across mutually exclusive services
intention_signals: It appears designed to provide a self-restarting, GPU-backed STT endpoint that is selectively enabled according to a system-wide VRAM budget.
novelty: The file explicitly documents a 16 GB VRAM ceiling and instructs that only one of three STT ears should be enabled, while also noting a GGUF runtime alternative.
contribution: This file contributes the runtime contract for the Granite STT ear: how it is started, which interpreter and script it uses, the port it serves, its expected VRAM footprint, and the constraints that govern when it should be enabled.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd user service file that starts the IBM Granite Speech 4.0 1b transformer STT ear on GPU at port 2033 with restart-on-failure and a 600-second startup timeout.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reliably launched, resource-bounded Granite-based speech-to-text endpoint within a multi-ear voice pipeline.
